indicators.name: bed
indicators.rationale: The bed is a significant obstacle to setting up a computer workstation, as it occupies a large portion of the space and is not suitable for prolonged sitting.

indicators.name: lack of flat personal work surface
indicators.rationale: There is no flat surface at sitting height, making it impossible to set up a computer workstation without external furniture.

indicators.name: low ceiling height
indicators.rationale: The low ceiling height may limit the ability to position a monitor at a comfortable viewing distance, potentially causing neck strain.

indicators.name: lack of ergonomic seating
indicators.rationale: There is no ergonomic seating available, which is crucial for maintaining correct posture during extended computer work.

indicators.name: decoration-heavy layout
indicators.rationale: The presence of decorative elements such as hanging lights and hanging plants suggests a more decorative than functional layout, which is not conducive to focused solo work.

reasoning_summary: The indoor scene is marginally adequate for computer work (solo). While there are some positive aspects such as natural light and a spacious layout, the presence of a bed and lack of flat personal work surface make it challenging to set up a comfortable and efficient workstation. The low ceiling height and lack of ergonomic seating further reduce the suitability for extended computer work.
